Prepare My Day · Auto-loaded
Acme Corp
$48K ARR·Aug 12 · 89 days·QBR · 9:00 AM
Where we are
Health 82, stable. Zero open CTAs. Strong enterprise adoption across IT and Engineering. David Kim confirmed SSO interest in last call — expansion is on the table.
Last Gong · May 10
David Kim unprompted mentioned enterprise tier by name. Asked about SSO rollout support and advanced reporting. Sentiment: strongly positive. No competitor mention.
The objective
Confirm value delivered in Q1, align on SSO pilot timeline, and get verbal commit on enterprise tier expansion before renewal in 89 days.

NovaVault
$31K ARR·Jun 1 · 17 DAYS 🔴·3:30 PM
Where we are
Health in freefall: 91→67→23. No Gong calls in 45 days. Previous champion James Wu left the company. New contact: Michael Torres.
Gong · 45 DAYS AGO 🔴
Last contact with James Wu who has since left. Michael Torres has had zero onboarding from us. This is a cold re-introduction.
The objective
Introduce yourself to Torres. Understand priorities. Buy time — request 60-day extension. This is a save play, not a renewal call.
Critical health (23) + new champion → emergency retention deck tailored for first-touch with Michael Torres

Meridian73 days dark
Meridian Health Systems
$22K ARR · Renews Jun 30
Situation Read
Why they went dark
Last positive Gong was March 15 — SSO rollout discussion. Gainsight shows adoption dropped 40% in April. Dust hypothesis: SSO rollout hit a technical blocker internally. No one flagged it to the CSM. They didn’t go dark because they’re unhappy — they went dark because they’re stuck.
Champion status
Jennifer Ramos (IT Manager) still appears active — she sent an inbound check-in email yesterday. Champion is intact.
Risk of wrong approach
Do not lead with renewal or commercial pressure. Jennifer is an IT stakeholder, not a buyer. Wrong email = she forwards to procurement and disengages personally.

Apex61 days dark
Apex Dynamics
$15K ARR · Renews Aug 1
Situation Read
Why they went dark
Champion change. James Wu (Head of IT) left the company. Michael Torres (IT Director) was introduced as the new contact but has had zero engagement with 1Password since the handover. The account went dark the moment James left.
Champion status — CRITICAL
James Wu gone. Ryan Patel is the new IT Director per LinkedIn update. Michael Torres may be a transitional contact only. Do not send a cold email to the wrong person.
Risk of wrong approach
Cold email to Ryan Patel with no warm intro is low-probability. Check AE relationship first. A CSM cold email to a new champion who has never heard of you can set the relationship back 90 days.
Champion Change Protocol
Step 1 · Check AE relationship
Does the AE own Apex? When did they last touch the account? Can they make a warm intro to Ryan Patel before the CSM reaches out cold?
Step 2 · LinkedIn check
Is Ryan Patel connected to anyone on the CS or AE team? A mutual connection changes the outreach approach entirely.
Step 3 · Recommended approach
If AE can intro: AE sends brief intro Slack/email, CSM follows within 24h referencing the intro.
If no AE relationship: CSM sends the cold intro below, leads with James Wu continuity.
Email signature applied to all email touches
Email signature
3-Touch Sequence
Re-engagement Intel
Champion change
James Wu → Ryan Patel
Risk level
HIGH — cold intro to unknown champion near renewal
Dust recommendation
Do not send cold email without AE warm intro attempt first
Timeline
Aug 1 renewal gives 61 days — enough time if re-engagement starts today

🗂

My Accounts

Every account in your book, fully loaded, one click away.
What this tab does

My Accounts is your full portfolio workspace. Search any account by name and load everything in one place: full Gmail thread history, Gong call recordings with direct links, Salesforce opportunity record, Gainsight account page, Ironclad contract status, and all stakeholder contacts with role and engagement history. No tab-switching between tools to understand one account. AI-generated account brief, success plan status, champion health, and expansion signals are surfaced alongside the raw data so you can form your own conclusions from complete information. You can log notes that sync to Gainsight, create tasks and CTAs, and launch any agent directly from the account record.

What it connects to
Gmail · Gong · Salesforce · Gainsight · Ironclad · Google Drive · Slack
Phase 2 feature · API connections required
📋

Success Plans

Every account’s success plan tracked and current across your entire book.
What this tab does

Success Plans is a portfolio view of where every account stands against their success milestones. At a glance you can see which accounts have current active plans, which plans are overdue for a refresh, and which accounts have no plan at all — a gap that directly impacts your Recipe for Success score. Clicking any account loads the live plan from Gainsight with milestone status, outstanding action items, and the next scheduled check-in date. Milestones can be updated and pushed back to Gainsight without leaving TeamOS. Dust flags plans that are misaligned with current health or renewal proximity and surfaces the specific update that would close the gap fastest. Admin-broadcast tasks related to success plan compliance land here automatically when CS leadership pushes a team-wide priority.

What it connects to
Gainsight · Google Drive · Gmail
Phase 2 feature · API connections required
👥

Team View

AE, BDR, CSM, and Onboarding all working the same account — without a single Slack thread.
What this tab does

Team View is the shared account collaboration layer. When a renewal is at risk or an expansion is in play, everyone who touches the account — Account Executive, Business Development Rep, Customer Success Manager, and Onboarding Specialist — sees the same account snapshot, the same signals, and the same next steps in one shared surface. Collaborative notes are attributed by role and timestamp. Task assignment works across roles: the CSM assigns the AE a warm intro, the AE requests the CSM join a call, OB flags a technical blocker for CS to resolve. The same admin task broadcast feature that CS leadership uses to push priorities to CSMs extends here — CS Ops can push account-specific tasks to the AE and BDR simultaneously. No more coordination overhead. One account, one view, every stakeholder aligned.

What it connects to
Gainsight · Salesforce · Slack · Gmail · Gong · Google Drive
Phase 2 feature · API connections required
New Campaign
Email signature

All campaign emails append this signature. Saved to localStorage and shared with Ghost-Buster.

📊

Analytics

How your book is trending — not just where it stands today.
What this tab does

Analytics shows the trends that matter over time, not just the current snapshot. Health score movement across your full book — which accounts are improving, which are declining, and at what rate. EBR coverage rate against your quarterly target with the specific accounts pulling the number down. Gong engagement frequency by account, correlated with renewal outcomes so you can see which cadences actually drive retention in your book specifically. Email response rates by account segment and outreach type. Recipe for Success score trend week-over-week so you can see whether your actions are moving the number. CSM activity patterns that reveal where you’re spending time versus where the risk actually is. CS leadership sees a team rollup. Each CSM sees their own book. The same admin guidance layer that injects priority rules into Prepare My Day will eventually surface as coaching insights here — flagging patterns in your data before your team lead flags them in a 1:1.

What it connects to
Gainsight · Gong · Salesforce · Gmail
Phase 2 feature · API connections required
